text: 英語 聽力 模擬 視題 七
第一節
聽下面五段對話
每段對話後有一個小題
從題中所給的
A B C 3個選項中選出最佳選項
聽完每段對話後
你都有十秒鐘的時間
來回答有關小題和閱讀下一小題
每段對話僅讀一遍
現在你有五秒鐘的時間
閱讀第一小題的有關內容
Hey Alex, you're late!
What happened?
Did you oversleep or get stuck in traffic?
Actually, the elevator in my building broke down this morning.
I was caught inside for thirty minutes.
Hi Mr. Smith, I need to request some time off next month.
What's the reason?
I'm getting married, and I'll need a week for the wedding and honeymoon.
Congratulations, just submit the leave application, and I'll approve it.
I'm really excited about making my new year's resolutions.
I think it's a great way to reflect and set goals for personal growth.
I don't see the point.
I've tried making new year's resolutions before, but I never stick to them.
It just feels like a waste of time.
Do you have any ideas for a housewarming gift for John?
How about a good quality set of knives?
John loves cooking dishes.
Sounds like a good idea.
I'm starving.
Let's go to the Chinese restaurant for dinner.
We always go there.
How about trying something different?
Maybe French or Italian food?
Italian food sounds good.
Hello, I want to buy a new phone.
Can you help me?
Of course.
What's the most important thing to you about a phone?
Camera quality, battery life, or maybe screen size?
I think I need a good camera because I like taking photos.
Got it.
This phone is great for taking photos in different lighting conditions.
Can I try it out?
Sure.
Feel free to test it out.
And if this one doesn't meet your expectations, we have several other options available.

Of course.

I think I need a good camera because I like taking photos.
Got it.

Feel free to test it out.

Now, you have 15 seconds to read the 3rd question.
Emma, you look puzzled.
What's up?
I've been cleaning my room, and I found a lot of old stuff I don't use anymore, like
clothes, books, and even an old guitar.
I'm not sure what to do with them.
Hmm.
Have you thought about selling them?
I did think about that, but some of the items aren't in good condition.
For example, the guitar is worn.
Maybe you could fix it and try selling it online at a discount.
That's true.
What about the clothes?
You could donate them to a charity.
It's a nice way to help others.
That's a great idea.
What about the books?
You could start a little free library in your neighborhood.
It's a cool way to share books with others.
A free library?
That sounds fun.
I'll look into it.

Based on my recent checkup, the doctor said, I need to exercise to improve my health.
Can you give me some suggestions?
Sure, you could try jogging.
It's easy to do and doesn't require any special equipment.
Work with 15 minutes a day and gradually increase the time.
That sounds manageable.
What else?
Another option is practicing yoga.
It works your entire body.
Practice yoga for at least 20 minutes a day.
There is a yoga studio near my house.
Any other suggestions?
Cycling is also a great way to burn calories.
You can start with 25 minutes and slowly increase the time.
Those all sound good.
I think I'll start with cycling, thanks for your suggestions.

聽下面一段對話回答第十四至第十七四個下題.
現在你有二十秒鐘的時間閱讀這四個下題.
Good afternoon, sir.
Welcome to Bright Coast Hotel.
What can I do for you?
I reserved a room online yesterday in the name of Tom Smith.
I'd like to check in now.
Let me check.
Well, I see you have a standard room reserved for two nights.
Is that correct?
Yes, that's right.
Here is my ID card.
All right.
Do you have any special requests or preferences for your stay?
I'd love a room with a balcony.
We do have a room with a balcony available, but it's a suite.
And there is an additional charge of $30 per night to upgrade.
It's a bit too expensive for me.
I'll stick with the standard room.
Okay, it's done.
You'll stay in room 502 on the fifth floor.
Here's your key card and ID card.
Thanks.
What time is breakfast served?
From 7 a.m. to 10 a.m. in our dining room on the first floor.
Perfect.
And is there Wi-Fi available?
Yes, we offer free Wi-Fi in all our rooms.
The network name is your room number and the password is the name of our hotel.
Got it.
Thank you.

Got it.
Thank you.
Now, you have 15 seconds to read these three questions.
Even as temperatures kept dropping in China's northeastern ice city of Harbeen, excitement
rose among a group of swimmers who enjoyed jumping into freezing water.
The daily event, known as ice swimming, is a winter tradition in Harbeen.
To create a pool in the Songhua River, the swimmers first had to break a 10-centimeter
thick cover of ice, then they removed the ice pieces, took off all the clothes except
the bathing suits, and prepared to dive in.
One by one, they jumped into the freezing 10-meter-long pool.
One of the swimmers was Chen Xia, from Jijiong Province.
She decided to jump into the river even though she had a cold.
Chen said the winter water where she lives is warmer than that in Harbeen.
She said the experience strengthened her confidence in winter swimming.
Yusha Feng lives in Harbeen.
The 61-year-old said winter swimming in the city dates back to the 1970s.
Later, in 1983, an official winter swimming group was established in Harbeen.
Yusha said she has felt a great sense of family and joy in the community during her 30 years
of winter swimming.

The second step is to the end.
There are two minutes of time now.
The answer to the test will be transferred to the Kegwantida Tika.
Let's hear the part to the end.
